NKJV.1: Do not be envious of evil men, Nor desire to be with them;
NKJV.2: For their heart devises violence, And their lips talk of troublemaking.
NKJV.3: Through wisdom a house is built, And by understanding it is established;
NKJV.4: By knowledge the rooms are filled With all precious and pleasant riches.
NKJV.5: A wise man is strong, Yes, a man of knowledge increases strength;
NKJV.6: For by wise counsel you will wage your own war, And in a multitude of counselors there is safety.
NKJV.7: Wisdom is too lofty for a fool; He does not open his mouth in the gate.
NKJV.8: He who plots to do evil Will be called a schemer.
NKJV.9: The devising of foolishness is sin, And the scoffer is an abomination to men.
NKJV.10: If you faint in the day of adversity, Your strength is small.
NKJV.11: Deliver those who are drawn toward death, And hold back those stumbling to the slaughter.
NKJV.12: If you say, “Surely we did not know this,” Does not He who weighs the hearts consider it? He who keeps your soul, does He not know it? And will He not render to each man according to his deeds?
NKJV.13: My son, eat honey because it is good, And the honeycomb which is sweet to your taste;
NKJV.14: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be to your soul; If you have found it, there is a prospect, And your hope will not be cut off.
NKJV.15: Do not lie in wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; Do not plunder his resting place;
NKJV.16: For a righteous man may fall seven times And rise again, But the wicked shall fall by calamity.
NKJV.17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, And do not let your heart be glad when he stumbles;
NKJV.18: Lest the LORD see it, and it displease Him, And He turn away His wrath from him.
NKJV.19: Do not fret because of evildoers, Nor be envious of the wicked;
NKJV.20: For there will be no prospect for the evil man; The lamp of the wicked will be put out.
NKJV.21: My son, fear the LORD and the king; Do not associate with those given to change;
NKJV.22: For their calamity will rise suddenly, And who knows the ruin those two can bring?
NKJV.23: These things also belong to the wise: It is not good to show partiality in judgment.
NKJV.24: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous,” Him the people will curse; Nations will abhor him.
NKJV.25: But those who rebuke the wicked will have delight, And a good blessing will come upon them.
NKJV.26: He who gives a right answer kisses the lips.
NKJV.27: Prepare your outside work, Make it fit for yourself in the field; And afterward build your house.
NKJV.28: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, For would you deceive with your lips?
NKJV.29: Do not say, “I will do to him just as he has done to me; I will render to the man according to his work.”
NKJV.30: I went by the field of the lazy man, And by the vineyard of the man devoid of understanding;
NKJV.31: And there it was, all overgrown with thorns; Its surface was covered with nettles; Its stone wall was broken down.
NKJV.32: When I saw it, I considered it well; I looked on it and received instruction:
NKJV.33: A little sleep, a little slumber, A little folding of the hands to rest;
NKJV.34: So shall your poverty come like a prowler, And your need like an armed man.

ESV.1: Be not envious of evil men, nor desire to be with them,
ESV.2: for their hearts devise violence, and their lips talk of trouble.
ESV.3: By wisdom a house is built, and by understanding it is established;
ESV.4: by knowledge the rooms are filled with all precious and pleasant riches.
ESV.5: A wise man is full of strength, and a man of knowledge enhances his might,
ESV.6: for by wise guidance you can wage your war, and in abundance of counselors there is victory.
ESV.7: Wisdom is too high for a fool; in the gate he does not open his mouth.
ESV.8: Whoever plans to do evil will be called a schemer.
ESV.9: The devising  of folly is sin, and the scoffer is an abomination to mankind.
ESV.10: If you faint in the day of adversity, your strength is small.
ESV.11: Rescue those who are being taken away to death; hold back those who are stumbling to the slaughter.
ESV.12: If you say, “Behold, we did not know this,” does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work?
ESV.13: My son, eat honey, for it is good, and the drippings of the honeycomb are sweet to your taste.
ESV.14: Know that wisdom is such to your soul; if you find it, there will be a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
ESV.15: Lie not in wait as a wicked man against the dwelling of the righteous; do no violence to his home;
ESV.16: for the righteous falls seven times and rises again, but the wicked stumble in times of calamity.
ESV.17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, and let not your heart be glad when he stumbles,
ESV.18: lest the LORD see it and be displeased, and turn away his anger from him.
ESV.19: Fret not yourself because of evildoers, and be not envious of the wicked,
ESV.20: for the evil man has no future; the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
ESV.21: My son, fear the LORD and the king, and do not join with those who do otherwise,
ESV.22: for disaster will arise suddenly from them, and who knows the ruin that will come from them both?
ESV.23: These also are sayings of the wise. Partiality in judging is not good.
ESV.24: Whoever says to the wicked, “You are in the right,” will be cursed by peoples, abhorred by nations,
ESV.25: but those who rebuke the wicked will have delight, and a good blessing will come upon them.
ESV.26: Whoever gives an honest answer kisses the lips.
ESV.27: Prepare your work outside; get everything ready for yourself in the field, and after that build your house.
ESV.28: Be not a witness against your neighbor without cause, and do not deceive with your lips.
ESV.29: Do not say, “I will do to him as he has done to me; I will pay the man back for what he has done.”
ESV.30: I passed by the field of a sluggard, by the vineyard of a man lacking sense,
ESV.31: and behold, it was all overgrown with thorns; the ground was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.
ESV.32: Then I saw and considered it; I looked and received instruction.
ESV.33: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,
ESV.34: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed man.

NASB.1: Do not be envious of evil men, Nor desire to be with them;
NASB.2: For their minds devise violence, And their lips talk of trouble.
NASB.3: By wisdom a house is built, And by understanding it is established;
NASB.4: And by knowledge the rooms are filled With all precious and pleasant riches.
NASB.5: A wise man is strong, And a man of knowledge increases power.
NASB.6: For by wise guidance you will wage war, And in abundance of counselors there is victory.
NASB.7: Wisdom is [too] exalted for a fool, He does not open his mouth in the gate.
NASB.8: One who plans to do evil, Men will call a schemer.
NASB.9: The devising of folly is sin, And the scoffer is an abomination to men.
NASB.10: If you are slack in the day of distress, Your strength is limited.
NASB.11: Deliver those who are being taken away to death, And those who are staggering to slaughter, Oh hold [them] back.
NASB.12: If you say, 'See, we did not know this,' Does He not consider [it] who weighs the hearts? And does He not know [it] who keeps your soul? And will He not render to man according to his work?
NASB.13: My son, eat honey, for it is good, Yes, the honey from the comb is sweet to your taste;
NASB.14: Know [that] wisdom is thus for your soul; If you find [it], then there will be a future, And your hope will not be cut off.
NASB.15: Do not lie in wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; Do not destroy his resting place;
NASB.16: For a righteous man falls seven times, and rises again, But the wicked stumble in [time of] calamity.
NASB.17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, And do not let your heart be glad when he stumbles;
NASB.18: Or the LORD will see [it] and be displeased, And turn His anger away from him.
NASB.19: Do not fret because of evildoers Or be envious of the wicked;
NASB.20: For there will be no future for the evil man; The lamp of the wicked will be put out.
NASB.21: My son, fear the LORD and the king; Do not associate with those who are given to change,
NASB.22: For their calamity will rise suddenly, And who knows the ruin [that comes] from both of them?
NASB.23: These also are sayings of the wise. To show partiality in judgment is not good.
NASB.24: He who says to the wicked, 'You are righteous,' Peoples will curse him, nations will abhor him;
NASB.25: But to those who rebuke the [wicked] will be delight, And a good blessing will come upon them.
NASB.26: He kisses the lips Who gives a right answer.
NASB.27: Prepare your work outside And make it ready for yourself in the field; Afterwards, then, build your house.
NASB.28: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, And do not deceive with your lips.
NASB.29: Do not say, 'Thus I shall do to him as he has done to me; I will render to the man according to his work.'
NASB.30: I passed by the field of the sluggard And by the vineyard of the man lacking sense,
NASB.31: And behold, it was completely overgrown with thistles; Its surface was covered with nettles, And its stone wall was broken down.
NASB.32: When I saw, I reflected upon it; I looked, [and] received instruction.
NASB.33: 'A little sleep, a little slumber, A little folding of the hands to rest,'
NASB.34: Then your poverty will come [as] a robber And your want like an armed man.

NLT.1:
Don’t envy evil people
or desire their company.
NLT.2:
For their hearts plot violence,
and their words always stir up trouble.
NLT.3:
A house is built by wisdom
and becomes strong through good sense.
NLT.4:
Through knowledge its rooms are filled
with all sorts of precious riches and valuables.
NLT.5:
The wise are mightier than the strong,
and those with knowledge grow stronger and stronger.
NLT.6:
So don’t go to war without wise guidance;
victory depends on having many advisers.
NLT.7:
Wisdom is too lofty for fools.
Among leaders at the city gate, they have nothing to say.
NLT.8:
A person who plans evil
will get a reputation as a troublemaker.
NLT.9:
The schemes of a fool are sinful;
everyone detests a mocker.
NLT.10:
If you fail under pressure,
your strength is too small.
NLT.11:
Rescue those who are unjustly sentenced to die;
save them as they stagger to their death.
NLT.12:
Don’t excuse yourself by saying, “Look, we didn’t know.”
For God understands all hearts, and he sees you.
He who guards your soul knows you knew.
He will repay all people as their actions deserve.
NLT.13:
My child, eat honey, for it is good,
and the honeycomb is sweet to the taste.
NLT.14:
In the same way, wisdom is sweet to your soul.
If you find it, you will have a bright future,
and your hopes will not be cut short.
NLT.15:
Don’t wait in ambush at the home of the godly,
and don’t raid the house where the godly live.
NLT.16:
The godly may trip seven times, but they will get up again.
But one disaster is enough to overthrow the wicked.
NLT.17:
Don’t rejoice when your enemies fall;
don’t be happy when they stumble.
NLT.18:
For the LORD will be displeased with you
and will turn his anger away from them.
NLT.19:
Don’t fret because of evildoers;
don’t envy the wicked.
NLT.20:
For evil people have no future;
the light of the wicked will be snuffed out.
NLT.21:
My child, fear the LORD and the king.
Don’t associate with rebels,
NLT.22:
for disaster will hit them suddenly.
Who knows what punishment will come
from the LORD and the king?
NLT.23: Here are some further sayings of the wise: It is wrong to show favoritism when passing judgment.
NLT.24:
A judge who says to the wicked, “You are innocent,”
will be cursed by many people and denounced by the nations.
NLT.25:
But it will go well for those who convict the guilty;
rich blessings will be showered on them.
NLT.26:
An honest answer
is like a kiss of friendship.
NLT.27:
Do your planning and prepare your fields
before building your house.
NLT.28:
Don’t testify against your neighbors without cause;
don’t lie about them.
NLT.29:
And don’t say, “Now I can pay them back for what they’ve done to me!
I’ll get even with them!”
NLT.30:
I walked by the field of a lazy person,
the vineyard of one with no common sense.
NLT.31:
I saw that it was overgrown with nettles.
It was covered with weeds,
and its walls were broken down.
NLT.32:
Then, as I looked and thought about it,
I learned this lesson:
NLT.33:
A little extra sleep, a little more slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest —
NLT.34:
then poverty will pounce on you like a bandit;
scarcity will attack you like an armed robber.

WEB.1: Don’t be envious of evil men; neither desire to be with them:
WEB.2: for their hearts plot violence, and their lips talk about mischief.
WEB.3: Through wisdom a house is built; by understanding it is established;
WEB.4: by knowledge the rooms are filled with all rare and beautiful treasure.
WEB.5: A wise man has great power; and a knowledgeable man increases strength;
WEB.6: for by wise guidance you wage your war; and victory is in many advisors.
WEB.7: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he doesn’t open his mouth in the gate.
WEB.8: One who plots to do evil will be called a schemer.
WEB.9: The schemes of folly are sin. The mocker is detested by men.
WEB.10: If you falter in the time of trouble, your strength is small.
WEB.11: Rescue those who are being led away to death! Indeed, hold back those who are staggering to the slaughter!
WEB.12: If you say, “Behold, we didn’t know this”; doesn’t he who weighs the hearts consider it? He who keeps your soul, doesn’t he know it? Shall he not render to every man according to his work?
WEB.13: My son, eat honey, for it is good; the droppings of the honeycomb, which are sweet to your taste:
WEB.14: so you shall know wisdom to be to your soul; if you have found it, then there will be a reward, your hope will not be cut off.
WEB.15: Don’t lay in wait, wicked man, against the habitation of the righteous. Don’t destroy his resting place:
WEB.16: for a righteous man falls seven times, and rises up again; but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.
WEB.17: Don’t rejoice when your enemy falls. Don’t let your heart be glad when he is overthrown;
WEB.18: lest Yahweh see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.
WEB.19: Don’t fret yourself because of evildoers; neither be envious of the wicked:
WEB.20: for there will be no reward to the evil man; and the lamp of the wicked shall be snuffed out.
WEB.21: My son, fear Yahweh and the king. Don’t join those who are rebellious:
WEB.22: for their calamity will rise suddenly; the destruction from them both — who knows?
WEB.23: These also are sayings of the wise. To show partiality in judgment is not good.
WEB.24: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous”; peoples will curse him, and nations will abhor him —
WEB.25: but it will go well with those who convict the guilty, and a rich blessing will come on them.
WEB.26: An honest answer is like a kiss on the lips.
WEB.27: Prepare your work outside, and get your fields ready. Afterwards, build your house.
WEB.28: Don’t be a witness against your neighbor without cause. Don’t deceive with your lips.
WEB.29: Don’t say, “I will do to him as he has done to me; I will render to the man according to his work.”
WEB.30: I went by the field of the sluggard, by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;
WEB.31: Behold, it was all grown over with thorns. Its surface was covered with nettles, and its stone wall was broken down.
WEB.32: Then I saw, and considered well. I saw, and received instruction:
WEB.33: a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep;
WEB.34: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your want as an armed man.

at the camp of the righteous man; ⓘ
don’t destroy his dwelling.
CSB17.16:
Though a righteous person falls seven times,
he will get up, ⓙ
but the wicked will stumble into ruin. ⓚ
CSB17.17:
Don’t gloat when your enemy falls,
and don’t let your heart rejoice when he stumbles, ⓛ
CSB17.18:
or the LORD will see, be displeased,
and turn his wrath away from him.
CSB17.19:
Don’t be agitated by evildoers,
and don’t envy the wicked. ⓜ
CSB17.20:
For the evil have no future; ⓝ
the lamp of the wicked will be put out. ⓐ
CSB17.21:
My son, fear the LORD, as well as the king, ⓑ
and don’t associate with rebels, [4]
CSB17.22:
for destruction will come suddenly from them;
who knows what distress these two can bring?
CSB17.23:
These sayings also belong to the wise: ⓒ
It is not good to show partiality in judgment. ⓓ
CSB17.24:
Whoever says to the guilty, “You are innocent” ⓔ  —
peoples will curse him, ⓕ and nations will denounce him;
CSB17.25:
but it will go well with those who convict the guilty,
and a generous blessing will come to them.
CSB17.26:
He who gives an honest answer
gives a kiss on the lips.
CSB17.27:
Complete your outdoor work, and prepare your field; ⓖ
afterward, build your house.
CSB17.28:
Don’t testify against your neighbor without cause. ⓗ
Don’t deceive with your lips.
CSB17.29:
Don’t say, “I’ll do to him what he did to me;
I’ll repay the man for what he has done.” ⓘ
CSB17.30:
I went by the field of a slacker
and by the vineyard of one lacking sense.
CSB17.31:
Thistles had come up everywhere,
weeds covered the ground, ⓙ
and the stone wall was ruined.
CSB17.32:
I saw, and took it to heart;
I looked, and received instruction:
CSB17.33:
a little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the arms to rest,
CSB17.34:
and your poverty will come like a robber,
and your need, like a bandit. ⓚ

AMP.1:
Do not be envious of evil men,
Nor desire to be with them;
AMP.2:
For their minds plot violence,
And their lips talk of trouble [for the innocent].
AMP.3:
Through [skillful and godly] wisdom a house [a life, a home, a family] is built,
And by understanding it is established [on a sound and good foundation],
AMP.4:
And by knowledge its rooms are filled
With all precious and pleasant riches.
AMP.5:
A wise man is strong,
And a man of knowledge strengthens his power;
AMP.6:
For by wise guidance you can wage your war,
And in an abundance of [wise] counselors there is victory and safety.
AMP.7:
Wisdom is too exalted for a [hardened, arrogant] fool;
He does not open his mouth in the gate [where the city's rulers sit in judgment].
AMP.8:
He who plans to do evil
Will be called a schemer or deviser of evil.
AMP.9:
The devising of folly is sin,
And the scoffer is repulsive to men.
AMP.10:
If you are slack (careless) in the day of distress,
Your strength is limited.
AMP.11:
Rescue those who are being taken away to death,
And those who stagger to the slaughter, Oh hold them back [from their doom]!
AMP.12:
If you [claim ignorance and] say, "See, we did not know this,"
Does He not consider it who weighs and examines the hearts and their motives?
And does He not know it who guards your life and keeps your soul?
And will He not repay [you and] every man according to his works?
AMP.13:
My son, eat honey, because it is good,
And the drippings of the honeycomb are sweet to your taste.
AMP.14:
Know that [skillful and godly] wisdom is [so very good] for your life and soul;
If you find wisdom, then there will be a future and a reward,
And your hope and expectation will not be cut off.
AMP.15:
Do not lie in wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous;
Do not destroy his resting place;
AMP.16:
For a righteous man falls seven times, and rises again,
But the wicked stumble in time of disaster and collapse.
AMP.17:
Do not rejoice and gloat when your enemy falls,
And do not let your heart be glad [in self-righteousness] when he stumbles,
AMP.18:
Or the LORD will see your gloating and be displeased,
And turn His anger away from your enemy.
AMP.19:
Do not get upset because of evildoers,
Or be envious of the wicked,
AMP.20:
For there will be no future for the evil man;
The lamp of the wicked will be put out.
AMP.21:
My son, fear the LORD and the king;
And do not associate with those who are given to change [of allegiance, and are revolutionary],
AMP.22:
For their tragedy will rise suddenly,
And who knows the punishment that both [the LORD and the king] will bring on the rebellious?
AMP.23:
These also are sayings of the wise:
To show partiality in judgment is not good.
AMP.24:
He who says to the wicked, "You are righteous,"
Peoples will curse him, nations will denounce him;
AMP.25:
But to those [honorable judges] who rebuke the wicked, it will go well with them and they will find delight,
And a good blessing will come upon them.
AMP.26:
He kisses the lips [and wins the hearts of people]
Who gives a right and straightforward answer.
AMP.27:
Prepare your work outside
And get it ready for yourself in the field;
Afterward build your house and establish a home.
AMP.28:
Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause,
And do not deceive with your lips [speak neither lies nor half-truths].
AMP.29:
Do not say, "I will do to him as he has done to me;
I will pay the man back for his deed."
AMP.30:
I went by the field of the lazy man,
And by the vineyard of the man lacking understanding and common sense;
AMP.31:
And, behold, it was all overgrown with thorns,
And nettles were covering its surface,
And its stone wall was broken down.
AMP.32:
When I saw, I considered it well;
I looked and received instruction.
AMP.33:
"Yet a little sleep, a little slumber,
A little folding of the hands to rest [and daydream],"
AMP.34:
Then your poverty will come as a robber,
And your want like an armed man.

MSG.1:
Don't envy bad people;
don't even want to be around them.
MSG.2:
All they think about is causing a disturbance;
all they talk about is making trouble.
MSG.3:
It takes wisdom to build a house,
and understanding to set it on a firm foundation;
MSG.4:
It takes knowledge to furnish its rooms
with fine furniture and beautiful draperies.
MSG.5:
It's better to be wise than strong;
intelligence outranks muscle any day.
MSG.6:
Strategic planning is the key to warfare;
to win, you need a lot of good counsel.
MSG.7:
Wise conversation is way over the head of fools;
in a serious discussion they haven't a clue.
MSG.8:
The person who's always cooking up some evil
soon gets a reputation as prince of rogues.
MSG.10:
If you fall to pieces in a crisis,
there wasn't much to you in the first place.
MSG.11:
Rescue the perishing;
don't hesitate to step in and help.
MSG.12:
If you say, "Hey, that's none of my business,"
will that get you off the hook?
Someone is watching you closely, you know—
Someone not impressed with weak excuses.
MSG.13:
Eat honey, dear child—it's good for you—
and delicacies that melt in your mouth.
MSG.14:
Likewise knowledge,
and wisdom for your soul—
Get that and your future's secured,
your hope is on solid rock.
MSG.15:
Don't interfere with good people's lives;
don't try to get the best of them.
MSG.16:
No matter how many times you trip them up,
God-loyal people don't stay down long;
Soon they're up on their feet,
while the wicked end up flat on their faces.
MSG.17:
Don't laugh when your enemy falls;
don't crow over his collapse.
MSG.18:
God might see, and become very provoked,
and then take pity on his plight.
MSG.19:
Don't bother your head with braggarts
or wish you could succeed like the wicked.
MSG.20:
Those people have no future at all;
they're headed down a dead-end street.
MSG.21:
Fear God, dear child—respect your leaders;
don't be defiant or mutinous.
MSG.22:
Without warning your life can turn upside-down,
and who knows how or when it might happen?
MSG.23:
It's wrong, very wrong,
to go along with injustice.
MSG.24:
Whoever whitewashes the wicked
gets a black mark in the history books,
MSG.25:
But whoever exposes the wicked
will be thanked and rewarded.
MSG.26:
An honest answer
is like a warm hug.
MSG.27:
First plant your fields;
then build your barn.
MSG.28:
Don't talk about your neighbors behind their backs—
no slander or gossip, please.
MSG.29:
Don't say to anyone, "I'll get back at you for what you did to me.
I'll make you pay for what you did!"
MSG.30:
One day I walked by the field of an old lazybones,
and then passed the vineyard of a lout;
MSG.31:
They were overgrown with weeds,
thick with thistles, all the fences broken down.
MSG.32:
I took a long look and pondered what I saw;
the fields preached me a sermon and I listened:
MSG.33:
"A nap here, a nap there, a day off here, a day off there,
sit back, take it easy—do you know what comes next?
MSG.34:
Just this: You can look forward to a dirt-poor life,
with poverty as your permanent houseguest!"

NIV.1:
Do not envy wicked men,
do not desire their company;
NIV.2:
for their hearts plot violence,
and their lips talk about making trouble.
NIV.3:
By wisdom a house is built,
and through understanding it is established;
NIV.4:
through knowledge its rooms are filled
with rare and beautiful treasures.
NIV.5:
A wise man has great power,
and a man of knowledge increases strength;
NIV.6:
for waging war you need guidance,
and for victory many advisers.
NIV.7:
Wisdom is too high for a fool;
in the assembly at the gate he has nothing to say.
NIV.8:
He who plots evil
will be known as a schemer.
NIV.9:
The schemes of folly are sin,
and men detest a mocker.
NIV.10:
If you falter in times of trouble,
how small is your strength!
NIV.11:
Rescue those being led away to death;
hold back those staggering toward slaughter.
NIV.12:
If you say, “But we knew nothing about this,”
does not he who weighs the heart perceive it?
Does not he who guards your life know it?
Will he not repay each person according to what he has done?
NIV.13:
Eat honey, my son, for it is good;
honey from the comb is sweet to your taste.
NIV.14:
Know also that wisdom is sweet to your soul;
if you find it, there is a future hope for you,
and your hope will not be cut off.
NIV.15:
Do not lie in wait like an outlaw against a righteous man’s house,
do not raid his dwelling place;
NIV.16:
for though a righteous man falls seven times, he rises again,
but the wicked are brought down by calamity.
NIV.17:
Do not gloat when your enemy falls;
when he stumbles, do not let your heart rejoice,
NIV.18:
or the Lord will see and disapprove
and turn his wrath away from him.
NIV.19:
Do not fret because of evil men
or be envious of the wicked,
NIV.20:
for the evil man has no future hope,
and the lamp of the wicked will be snuffed out.
NIV.21:
Fear the Lord and the king, my son,
and do not join with the rebellious,
NIV.22:
for those two will send sudden destruction upon them,
and who knows what calamities they can bring?
NIV.23:
Further Sayings of the Wise
These also are sayings of the wise:
To show partiality in judging is not good:
NIV.24:
Whoever says to the guilty, “You are innocent”—
peoples will curse him and nations denounce him.
NIV.25:
But it will go well with those who convict the guilty,
and rich blessing will come upon them.
NIV.26:
An honest answer
is like a kiss on the lips.
NIV.27:
Finish your outdoor work
and get your fields ready;
after that, build your house.
NIV.28:
Do not testify against your neighbor without cause,
or use your lips to deceive.
NIV.29:
Do not say, “I’ll do to him as he has done to me;
I’ll pay that man back for what he did.”
NIV.30:
I went past the field of the sluggard,
past the vineyard of the man who lacks judgment;
NIV.31:
thorns had come up everywhere,
the ground was covered with weeds,
and the stone wall was in ruins.
NIV.32:
I applied my heart to what I observed
and learned a lesson from what I saw:
NIV.33:
A little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest—
NIV.34:
and poverty will come on you like a bandit
and scarcity like an armed man.

NRSVCE.1:
Do not envy the wicked,
nor desire to be with them;
NRSVCE.2:
for their minds devise violence,
and their lips talk of mischief.
NRSVCE.3:
By wisdom a house is built,
and by understanding it is established;
NRSVCE.4:
by knowledge the rooms are filled
with all precious and pleasant riches.
NRSVCE.5:
Wise warriors are mightier than strong ones,
and those who have knowledge than those who have strength;
NRSVCE.6:
for by wise guidance you can wage your war,
and in abundance of counselors there is victory.
NRSVCE.7:
Wisdom is too high for fools;
in the gate they do not open their mouths.
NRSVCE.8:
Whoever plans to do evil
will be called a mischief-maker.
NRSVCE.9:
The devising of folly is sin,
and the scoffer is an abomination to all.
NRSVCE.10:
If you faint in the day of adversity,
your strength being small;
NRSVCE.11:
if you hold back from rescuing those taken away to death,
those who go staggering to the slaughter;
NRSVCE.12:
if you say, “Look, we did not know this” —
does not he who weighs the heart perceive it?
Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it?
And will he not repay all according to their deeds?
NRSVCE.13:
My child, eat honey, for it is good,
and the drippings of the honeycomb are sweet to your taste.
NRSVCE.14:
Know that wisdom is such to your soul;
if you find it, you will find a future,
and your hope will not be cut off.
NRSVCE.15:
Do not lie in wait like an outlaw against the home of the righteous;
do no violence to the place where the righteous live;
NRSVCE.16:
for though they fall seven times, they will rise again;
but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.
NRSVCE.17:
Do not rejoice when your enemies fall,
and do not let your heart be glad when they stumble,
NRSVCE.18:
or else the LORD will see it and be displeased,
and turn away his anger from them.
NRSVCE.19:
Do not fret because of evildoers.
Do not envy the wicked;
NRSVCE.20:
for the evil have no future;
the lamp of the wicked will go out.
NRSVCE.21:
My child, fear the LORD and the king,
and do not disobey either of them;
NRSVCE.22:
for disaster comes from them suddenly,
and who knows the ruin that both can bring?
NRSVCE.23:
These also are sayings of the wise:
Partiality in judging is not good.
NRSVCE.24:
Whoever says to the wicked, “You are innocent,”
will be cursed by peoples, abhorred by nations;
NRSVCE.25:
but those who rebuke the wicked will have delight,
and a good blessing will come upon them.
NRSVCE.26:
One who gives an honest answer
gives a kiss on the lips.
NRSVCE.27:
Prepare your work outside,
get everything ready for you in the field;
and after that build your house.
NRSVCE.28:
Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause,
and do not deceive with your lips.
NRSVCE.29:
Do not say, “I will do to others as they have done to me;
I will pay them back for what they have done.”
NRSVCE.30:
I passed by the field of one who was lazy,
by the vineyard of a stupid person;
NRSVCE.31:
and see, it was all overgrown with thorns;
the ground was covered with nettles,
and its stone wall was broken down.
NRSVCE.32:
Then I saw and considered it;
I looked and received instruction.
NRSVCE.33:
A little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest,
NRSVCE.34:
and poverty will come upon you like a robber,
and want, like an armed warrior.

NET.1: Do not envy evil people, do not desire to be with them;
NET.2: for their hearts contemplate violence, and their lips speak harm.
NET.3: By wisdom a house is built, and through understanding it is established;
NET.4: by knowledge its rooms are filled with all kinds of precious and pleasing treasures.
NET.5: A wise warrior is strong, and a man of knowledge makes his strength stronger;
NET.6: for with guidance you wage your war, and with numerous advisers there is victory.
NET.7: Wisdom is unattainable for a fool; in court he does not open his mouth.
NET.8: The one who plans to do evil will be called a scheming person.
NET.9: A foolish scheme is sin, and the scorner is an abomination to people.
NET.10: If you faint in the day of trouble, your strength is small!
NET.11: Deliver those being taken away to death, and hold back those slipping to the slaughter.
NET.12: If you say, “But we did not know about this,” does not the one who evaluates hearts consider? Does not the one who guards your life know? Will he not repay each person according to his deeds?
NET.13: Eat honey, my child, for it is good, and honey from the honeycomb is sweet to your taste.
NET.14: Likewise, know that wisdom is sweet to your soul; if you find it, you will have a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
NET.15: Do not lie in wait like the wicked against the place where the righteous live; do not assault his home.
NET.16: Although a righteous person may fall seven times, he gets up again, but the wicked will be brought down by calamity.
NET.17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, and when he stumbles do not let your heart rejoice,
NET.18: lest the LORD see it, and be displeased, and turn his wrath away from him.
NET.19: Do not fret because of evil people or be envious of wicked people,
NET.20: for the evil person has no future, and the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished.
NET.21: Fear the LORD, my child, as well as the king, and do not associate with rebels,
NET.22: for suddenly their destruction will overtake them, and who knows the ruinous judgment both the LORD and the king can bring?
NET.23: These sayings also are from the wise: To show partiality in judgment is terrible:
NET.24: The one who says to the guilty, “You are innocent,” peoples will curse him, and nations will denounce him.
NET.25: But there will be delight for those who convict the guilty, and a pleasing blessing will come on them.
NET.26: Like a kiss on the lips is the one who gives an honest answer.
NET.27: Establish your work outside and get your fields ready; afterward build your house.
NET.28: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, and do not deceive with your words.
NET.29: Do not say, “I will do to him just as he has done to me; I will pay him back according to what he has done.”
NET.30: I passed by the field of a sluggard, by the vineyard of one who lacks wisdom.
NET.31: I saw that thorns had grown up all over it, the ground was covered with weeds, and its stone wall was broken down.
NET.32: When I saw this, I gave careful consideration to it; I received instruction from what I saw:
NET.33: “A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to relax,
NET.34: and your poverty will come like a bandit, and your need like an armed robber.”
